title: Block Yield
description: Define a method that takes a block and uses the 'yield' keyword to execute it.
problemStatement:
Method with a hole! Define a method named `run_block`. Inside the method, print `"Start"`, then `yield` to the block, and finally print `"End"`.

**Requirement:** Call the method and pass a block `{ puts "Inside" }`.
hints: `yield` tells Ruby to 'go run the code that was passed to me as a block right now'.
solutionCode:
def run_block
  puts "Start"
  yield
  puts "End"
end

run_block { puts "Inside" }
